setsockopt

Sets a socket option.

int setsockopt(
    SOCKET s,
    int level,
    int optname,
    const char FAR  *optval,
    int optlen
);

Parameters

s
[in] Descriptor identifying a socket.
level
[in] Level at which the option is defined; the supported levels are SOL_SOCKET and IPPROTO_TCP.
optname
[in] Socket option for which the value is to be set.
optval
[in] Pointer to the buffer in which the value for the requested option is supplied.
optlen
[in] Size of the optval buffer.

Return Values

If no error occurs, setsockopt returns zero. Otherwise, a value of SOCKET_ERROR is returned, and a specific error code can be retrieved by calling WSAGetLastError.

Error code Meaning
WSANOTINITIALISED A successful WSAStartup call must occur before using this function.
WSAEINPROGRESS A blocking call is in progress, or the service provider is still processing a callback function.
WSAEINVAL level is not valid, or the information in optval is not valid.
WSAENOPROTOOPT The option is unknown or unsupported for the specified provider or socket
WSAENOTSOCK The descriptor is not a socket.

Remarks

The setsockopt function sets the current value for a socket option associated with a socket of any type, in any state. Although options can exist at multiple protocol levels, they are always present at the uppermost socket level. Options affect socket operations, such as whether broadcast messages can be sent on the socket.

Note   If the setsockopt function is called before the bind function, TCP/IP options will not be checked with TCP/IP until the bind occurs. In this case, the setsockopt function call will always succeed, but the bind function call may fail because of an early setsockopt failing.

There are two types of socket options: Boolean options that enable or disable a feature or behavior, and options that require an integer value or structure. To enable a Boolean option, optval points to a nonzero integer. To disable the option optval points to an integer equal to zero. The optlen parameter should be equal to sizeof(int) for Boolean options. For other options, optval points to the an integer or structure that contains the desired value for the option, and optlen is the length of the integer or structure.

The following options are supported for setsockopt. For default values of these options, see the description. The Type identifies the type of data addressed by optval.

level = SOL_SOCKET

Value Type Meaning
SO_BROADCAST BOOL Allows transmission of broadcast messages on the socket. Only UDP sockets can be configured as broadcast sockets.
SO_DONTLINGER BOOL Does not block close waiting for unsent data to be sent. Setting this option is equivalent to setting SO_LINGER with l_onoff set to zero.
SO_EXCLUSIVEADDRUSE BOOL Enables a socket to be bound for exclusive access.
SO_LINGER structure LINGER Lingers on close if unsent data is present.
SO_RCVBUF int Specifies the total per-socket buffer space reserved for receives. This is unrelated to SO_MAX_MSG_SIZE or the size of a TCP window.
SO_REUSEADDR BOOL Allows the socket to be bound to an address that is already in use. (See bind.)
SO_SNDBUF int Specifies the total per-socket buffer space reserved for sends. This is unrelated to SO_MAX_MSG_SIZE or the size of a TCP window.
SO_SNDTIMEO int Send time-out.
SO_RCVTIMEO int Receive time-out.

level = IPPROTO_TCP

Value Type Meaning
TCP_NODELAY BOOL Disables the Nagle algorithm for send coalescing.

SO_LINGER
The SO_LINGER option controls the action taken when unsent data is queued on a socket and a closesocket is performed. See closesocket for a description of the way in which the SO_LINGER settings affect the semantics of closesocket. The title sets the desired behavior by creating a LINGER structure (pointed to by the optval parameter) with the members l_onoff and l_linger set appropriately.
SO_REUSEADDR
By default, a socket cannot be bound (see bind) to a local address that is already in use. On occasion, however, it can be necessary to reuse an address in this way. Since every connection is uniquely identified by the combination of local and remote addresses, there is no problem with having two sockets bound to the same local address as long as the remote addresses are different. To inform the Winsock provider that a bind on a socket should not be disallowed because the desired address is already in use by another socket, the title should set the SO_REUSEADDR socket option for the socket before issuing the bind. The option is interpreted only at the time of the bind. It is therefore unnecessary and harmless to set the option on a socket that is not to be bound to an existing address. Setting or resetting the option after the bind has no effect on this or any other socket.
SO_RCVBUF and SO_SNDBUF
Titles can use these options to request different buffer sizes (larger or smaller). The call to setsockopt can succeed even when the implementation did not provide the whole amount requested. A title must call getsockopt with the same option to check the buffer size actually provided.
TCP_NODELAY
Always used on Xbox.

BSD options not supported for setsockopt are:

Value Type Meaning
SO_DEBUG BOOL Records debugging information.
SO_DONTROUTE BOOL Does not route: sends directly to interface.
SO_ERROR int Retrieves and clears the error status.
SO_KEEPALIVE BOOL Sends keep-alives.
SO_OOBINLINE BOOL Receives OOB data in the normal data stream.
SO_RCVLOWAT int Receives low watermark.
SO_SNDLOWAT int Sends low watermark.
TCP_MAXSEG int Receives TCP maximum-segment size.

Other options not supported for setsockopt are:

Value Type Meaning
SO_ACCEPTCONN BOOL Socket is listening. Only usable in getsockopt.
SO_CONDITIONAL_ACCEPT BOOL Enables sockets to delay the acknowledgment of a connection.
SO_GROUP_ID GROUP Reserved.
SO_GROUP_PRIORITY int Reserved.
SO_MAX_MSG_SIZE unsigned int Maximum size of a message for message-oriented socket types (for example, SOCK_DGRAM). Has no meaning for stream oriented sockets.
SO_PROTOCOL_INFO structure Description of protocol information for protocol that is bound to this socket.
SO_TYPE int Type of the socket. Only usable in getsockopt.
PVD_CONFIG varies An opaque data structure object from the service provider associated with socket s.

Requirements

Header: Declared in winsockx.h, for include use Xtl.h.
Import Library: Use Xnet.lib for titles that only support system link play, XOnline.lib for titles that support online play. Use the secure versions of the library file (Xnets.lib or XOnlines.lib) for final testing.

See Also

Winsock Overview, Winsock Functions | bind, getsockopt, ioctlsocket, socket
